get.summary: Returns a list of lakehouses from the specified workspace.
get.description:
This API supports [pagination](/rest/api/fabric/articles/pagination).

## Permissions

 The caller must have a *viewer* workspace role. 

## Required Delegated Scopes 

Workspace.Read.All or Workspace.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Lists items in a folder and its nested folders, or just a folder only. True - All items in the folder and its nested folders are listed, False - Only items in the folder are listed. The default value is true.

parameters.name: rootFolderId
parameters.description: This parameter allows users to filter items based on a specific root folder. If not provided, the workspace is used as the root folder.

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: A token for retrieving the next page of results.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* InvalidItemType - Invalid item type

post.summary: Creates a lakehouse in the specified workspace.
post.description:
This API supports [long running operations (LRO)](/rest/api/fabric/articles/long-running-operation).



 To create lakehouse with a public definition, refer to [Lakehouse definition](/rest/api/fabric/articles/item-management/definitions/lakehouse-definition) article.

 ## Permissions

 The caller must have a *contributor* workspace role.

## Required Delegated Scopes 

 Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

202.description: Request accepted, lakehouse provisioning in progress.

Location.description: The URL of the operation status, which can be used to track the operation state.

x-ms-operation-id.description: The operation ID which can be used with long running operations (LRO) APIs to track the operation state and get the result.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* InvalidItemType - Item type is invalid

* ItemDisplayNameAlreadyInUse - Item display name is already used. 

* CorruptedPayload - The provided payload is corrupted. 

* WorkspaceItemsLimitExceeded - Workspace item limit exceeded.

get.summary: Returns properties of the specified lakehouse.
get.description:
## Permissions

 The caller must have *read* permissions for the lakehouse. 

## Required Delegated Scopes 

 Lakehouse.Read.All or Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.Read.All or Item.ReadWrite.All

## Interface

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* ItemNotFound - The requested item was not found.

## Required Delegated Scopes 

 Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.ReadWrite.All

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

delete.description:
## Permissions

 The caller must have *write* permissions for the lakehouse. 

## Required Delegated Scopes 

 Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: hardDelete
parameters.description: Specifies whether to perform a hard delete. When set to `true`, the item is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. When set to `false` or not specified, the item is soft-deleted if the item type supports it.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* ItemNotFound - The requested item was not found.

* InsufficientWorkspaceRole - User doesn't have sufficient workspace role.

* FeatureNotAvailable - This could be due to the soft deletion feature not being available.

* UnsupportedItemType - This could be due to the soft deletion feature not supported by the requested item type.

* TenantSwitchDisabled - This could be due to the soft deletion feature being disabled by the tenant admin.

* InvalidTargetItemStateForSoftDeletion - The item is in invalid states for soft deletion.

* InvalidParentItemStateForSoftDeletion - The item's parent item is not in Active state.

post.description:
> [!NOTE]
> This API is part of a Preview release and is provided for evaluation and development purposes only. It may change based on feedback and is not recommended for production use.
> The URL for this API has been updated to include the job type as part of the path, replacing the previous use of a query parameter. For backward compatibility, invocations using the query parameter are still supported.

## Required Delegated Scopes 

Lakehouse.Execute.All or Item.Execute.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: runOnDemandTableMaintenanceRequest
parameters.description: Run on-demand table maintenance request payload.

202.description: Request accepted, job execution is in progress.

Location.description: The URL to the job instance resource.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* MissingMinimalPermissions - The caller does not have sufficient permissions to run job instance. 

* InvalidJobType - The requested job type is invalid. 

* TooManyRequestsForJobs - The caller is spamming by lots of run on-demand job requests. 

* ItemNotFound - Requested item id not found.

post.summary: Create a new Refresh MaterializedLakeViews schedule for a lakehouse.
post.description:
> [!NOTE]
> This API is part of a Preview release and is provided for evaluation and development purposes only. It may change based on feedback and is not recommended for production use.

## Required Delegated Scopes: 

(Lakehouse.Execute.All or Item.Execute.All) and (Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.ReadWrite.All)

## Limitations 
- A lakehouse can create maximum 20 schedulers.
- Materialized Lake View supports only one active refresh schedule per lineage.

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | No |

## Interface

parameters.name: createScheduleRequest
parameters.description: A lakehouse refresh materialized lake views schedule create request.

Location.description: The URL of the newly created schedule.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* InsufficientPrivileges - The caller does not have sufficient permissions to access the item

* ItemNotFound - Requested item id not found

* ScheduleExceedsLimit - The number of created schedules for the item exceeds the limit

get.summary: Returns a list of livy sessions from the specified item identifier.
get.description:
This API supports [pagination](/rest/api/fabric/articles/pagination).

## Permissions

 The caller must have *read* permissions for the lakehouse. 

## Required Delegated Scopes 

 Lakehouse.Read.All or Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.Read.All or Item.ReadWrite.All

## Interface

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to retrieve the next page of results, if available.

200.description: OK -- Returns a list of all livy sessions associated to an itemId.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:

* SparkCoreItemNotFound - Lakehouse identifier does not exist

get.summary: Returns a list of livy sessions from the specified item identifier (beta).
get.description:
[!NOTE]
> This API is part of a Beta release and is provided for evaluation and development purposes only. It may change based on feedback and is not recommended for production use.

**When calling this API, callers must specify `true` as the value for the query parameter `beta`.**

 This API supports [pagination](/rest/api/fabric/articles/pagination).

## Permissions

 The caller must have *read* permissions for the lakehouse. 

## Required Delegated Scopes 

 Lakehouse.Read.All or Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.Read.All or Item.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: beta
parameters.description: This required parameter must be set to `true` to access this API, which is currently in beta.

parameters.name: submittedDateTime
parameters.description: Submitted date time filter. Filter the sessions based on the submitted date time in UTC, using the YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ format.

parameters.name: endDateTime
parameters.description: Ended date time filter. Filter the sessions based on the ended date time in UTC, using the YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ format.

parameters.name: submitter.Id
parameters.description: The GUID value of the user who submitted the session.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The state of the session. Possible values are: `NotStarted`, `InProgress`, `Cancelled`, `Failed`, `Succeeded`, `Unknown`. This value maps to the `state` field in the response.

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to retrieve the next page of results, if available.

200.description: OK -- Returns a list of all livy sessions associated to an itemId.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* SparkCoreItemNotFound - Lakehouse identifier does not exist

* SparkCoreUnexpectedException - Unhandled exception ocurred.

post.summary: Returns the specified lakehouse public definition.
post.description:
This API supports [long running operations (LRO)](/rest/api/fabric/articles/long-running-operation).

When you get a lakehouse's public definition, the sensitivity label is not a part of the definition.

## Permissions

The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the lakehouse.

## Required Delegated Scopes

 Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.ReadWrite.All 

## Limitations

 This API is blocked for a lakehouse with an encrypted sensitivity label.

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format of the lakehouse public definition.

202.description: Request accepted. Retrieving the definition is in progress.

Location.description: The URL of the operation status, which can be used to track the operation state.

x-ms-operation-id.description: The operation ID which can be used with long running operations (LRO) APIs to track the operation state and get the result.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* OperationNotSupportedForItem - Operation not supported for requested item.

post.summary: Overrides the definition for the specified lakehouse.
post.description:
This API supports [long running operations (LRO)](/rest/api/fabric/articles/long-running-operation).

Updating the lakehouse's definition does not affect its sensitivity label.

## Permissions

The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the lakehouse. 
## Required Delegated Scopes

 Lakehouse.ReadWrite.All or Item.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: updateMetadata
parameters.description: When set to true and the .platform file is provided as part of the definition, the item's metadata is updated using the metadata in the .platform file

202.description: Request accepted. Update definition is in progress.

Location.description: The URL of the operation status, which can be used to track the operation state.

x-ms-operation-id.description: The operation ID which can be used with long running operations (LRO) APIs to track the operation state and get the result.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* OperationNotSupportedForItem - Operation not supported for requested item. 

* CorruptedPayload - The provided payload is corrupted.
